observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare and submit the BRS for CY 2022 for the GF and record the adjustments for all reconciling items identified in in the BRS for the SEF and TF.

Henceforth, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare the BRS within ten (10) days after receipt of the BS and submit the same monthly to the Auditor, pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the concerned personnel to:

a)	Submit the following reports/documents:

•	Status Report of the Project Implementation, pertaining to the fund transfer received by the Municipality as of December 31, 2022 and other documentary evidence as requested last February 2, 2023; 

•	Subsidiary ledgers of the accounts Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs as of December 31, 2022; and

•	Annual Investment Plan and Supplemental Investment Plan for Odette Fund transfer duly approved by the Sanggunian.

b)	Remit/refund the unused balance of fund transfers to the following:


c)	Verify the status of the undeposited check amounting to ₱200,000.00, for the implementation of the project for the Teen Center of San Rafael National High School;

d)	Reconcile the accounts Due to NGAs and Due to other LGUs and identify the details of the unreconciled balances and negative balance of the Due to other LGUs account; 

e)	Submit a justification on the discrepancies noted between the amount received by the beneficiary of Typhoon Odette fund transfer and the Certificate of Eligibility; and

f)	Make the necessary adjusting entries in the erroneous recording of the transactions noted upon reconciliation.

Henceforth, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure that the liquidation report of the fund transfers with all the originals of vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents be submitted to the Audit Team as prescribed under COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:

a)	Submit the detailed schedule of Accounts Payable as of December 31, 2022 and other documents to support the claims against government funds;
	
b)	Adhere to the provision of Section 141, 145 and 151, Volume II of the MNGAS for LGUs in the recognition of the unpaid claims of the Municipality as of year-end and the retention money deducted from the progress billings of various contractors; and

c)	Adhere to the provisions of Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445 and the pertinent provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs relative to the supporting documents necessary in the recording of Accounts Payable of the Municipality.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineering, ensure that the 20% DF be optimally utilized by implementing the various PPAs included in the AIP to help achieve the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the Municipality for the benefit of its constituents.

We likewise recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Development Council identify those fully completed PPAs with unutilized balance and PPAs that could not be implemented, and reprogram the same to other social, economic or environmental PPAs that will help uplift the lives of its constituents.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

OIC-Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent RPT by conducting more aggressive tax campaign and by strictly implementing the remedies provided in Sections 256 to 260 of R.A. No. 7160; and

OIC-Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, to conduct assessment of all real properties in the Municipality, determine those properties that belong to the government for cleansing, and correct the possible duplication of records.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, fast track the update and revision of the Municipal Revenue Code in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and DILG-DOF JMC No. 2019-01.

We also reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all imposed taxes, charges, and fees are supported with duly enacted revenue ordinances.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to reconcile their records relative to the LDRRMF-STF balance and identify the amount included therein that were allotted for Capital Outlay expenditures.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to make the following adjustments in the Financial Statements:

Transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the DRRMF-MOOE and QRF under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books; 

   
Revert the unutilized LDRRMF for CY 2017 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the Sangguniang Bayan; and

Reclassify the fund transfer received for Typhoon Odette from Trust Liability-DRRM to Due to NGAs.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO and other concerned municipal officials to determine the priority PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF and ensure its full implementation and proper utilization in accordance with the guidelines set under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in order to address and strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

xxx

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of the MDRRM Council, set up an emergency responder team and ensure that the planned PPAs for disaster risk reduction and management are fully implemented to achieve the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

xxx

c.	Maintain a separate bank account for the LDRRMF where all the accumulated unutilized funds will be deposited so that in case adversities may befall, the Municipality’s needs shall be fully funded by the available cash under the LDRRMF in the Trust Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to submit on time all the records of transactions with supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant-Designate so as to facilitate the timely preparation of the financial statements and other required reports.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit to the Auditor the Financial Statements, supporting schedules, and other financial reports of the Municipality within the prescribed period as required by PD No. 1445 to aptly verify and examine the financial condition and performance of the Municipality as basis for rendering the appropriate audit opinion.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare the Notes to FS in accordance with the pertinent provisions of IPSAS 1 and COA Circulars 2016-004 and make certain the propriety and completeness of required disclosures in order to properly support the accounts presented financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant - Designate submit the lacking paid DVs with supporting documents totaling ₱1.333 million, proof of cancellation of 127 checks, the status of 293 checks not included in the RCI but was included in the check series issued to the Municipality and the eight checks with incomplete data in the Report of Checks issued, as shown in Annex A.1 and A.2, including the bank statements and Bank Reconciliation Statements for verification of the accuracy of the amount reported in the RCI.

Henceforth, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant-Designate ensure that the submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents thereto is made promptly and observe strictly the provisions of Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445 and the pertinent provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit the lacking supporting documents enumerated in Appendix Y to establish the propriety and validity of the transactions. 

Further, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned personnel justify:

The discrepancies in the amount indicated in the Minutes of Bid Opening, Abstract of Bid and BAC resolution recommending approval and Award for the Contract of the Rehabilitation/ Improvement of Municipal Driveway (Phase 2); and

The use of Direct Contracting as an alternative mode of procurement in the procurement of dental supplies in the Municipality.

Henceforth, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant – Designate ensure that all disbursements must be supported with adequate documents and the details of the post-qualification process undertaken by the Municipality be provided in the Post Qualification Evaluation Summary Report in order to determine whether the bidder complied with the requirements specified in the bidding documents so as to avoid issuance of the Notice of Suspension.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct all the concerned Department Heads to formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations embodied in the previous years’ audit report.

We likewise recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipality prepare and submit the duly accomplished AAPSI with supporting documents to the Auditor on the prescribed timeline, and likewise furnish the DBM, the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a copy of the said report and ensure that said status report is posted in the Municipality’s website.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person and other concerned municipal officials and employees to ensure the full implementation of GAD PPAs identified, maximize the utilization of the allocated budget for GAD, and ensure that the relevant GAD PPAs will address/respond to the gender-related issues identified in the Municipality.

We recommended the following:
	 
The Municipal Treasurer to account for all her undeposited collections and deposit the same with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB);

Henceforth, the concerned accountable officers to deposit all collections intact and in full amount with the authorized depository bank in accordance with the policies and procedures under PD No. 1445 and NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

The Municipal Treasurer to update and accomplish properly her cashbooks by providing the complete details of references, and shall be reconciled regularly with the accounting records, at least once every quarter;

The Municipal Accountant - Designate, Municipal Treasurer and Designated Liquidating Officer to reconcile their records and effect necessary corrections, if any, to present the correct balances of the Cash Local Treasury in the financial statements; and

The Municipal Accountant-Designate to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the accounts of all collecting/ accountable officers and reconcile these with their records regularly.





We also recommended the conduct of an investigation and take appropriate action on the issuance of ORs with various dates in December 2015, but appeared accomplished only after several ORs were issued in February 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents and the cancelled checks to the Auditor, and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory and Appraisal Committee to accurately identify all the lands owned by the Municipality and provide proper valuation thereof to correctly record the Land account in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to process the return of the unutilized balance of BGCM fund amounting to ₱6.658 million to the Bureau of Treasury.




We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned officials to comply with the posting and reporting requirements on the utilization of BGCM fund as mentioned in the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125.

We further recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineering Office ensure that the proposed program under the BGCM be implemented as soon as possible to timely deliver the vital services needed in addressing the crisis caused by the COVID-19 pandemic in the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the municipal officials and employees concerned to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001, by submitting the scanned copies of contracts, purchase orders, with complete details and supporting documents via       e-mail to the Auditor within five working days from the execution thereof, and submit the hard copy afterwards.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned payees to refund the excess amount of the COVID-19 Active Hazard Duty Pay and Special Risk Allowance totaling ₱12,181.13 they respectively received and henceforth ensure that payments are made within the prescribed limits to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor propose to the Sanggunian the creation of the mandatory positions in the MDRRMO and to provide funds thereof in accordance with the guidelines and provisions set by NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer properly accomplish, update daily and certify all her cashbooks pursuant to Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units, Volume II to ascertain the correctness and completeness of the transactions recorded thereon.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor provide a separate room or safe cubicle for the Liquidating Officer and Disbursing Officer, respectively. The partition of which must be appropriately enclosed and given corresponding lock and must ensure that only authorized personnel are allowed to have access to the room in order to protect government funds and property against risk of loss through theft.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant-Designate and Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents and the cancelled checks to the Auditor, and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended and the management agreed to submit the liquidation reports and necessary supporting documents to the IPG for the fund transfers received that are already due for liquidation and refund the unused or excess amount in compliance with the MOA.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to analyze, reconcile and identify/trace the details of transactions debited or credited to Government Equity and prepare the adjusting entries to close its balance and recognize the appropriate liability account/s.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to verify the validity and existence of the Inter-agency Receivables, and determine its composition, so that necessary adjusting entries can be taken up in the Municipality’s books of accounts and appropriate actions on the collection of these receivables can be made, if identified.




We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort to locate the subsidiary ledgers for Due to NGAs maintained by her predecessor to support the correctness and reliability of said account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:

Review/analyze prior years’ transactions pertaining to unidentified discrepancies, prepare a reconciliation report and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts; and

Conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments of reconciling items periodically, as identified and substantiated, to prevent existence of long outstanding unadjusted reconciling items.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit all the paid DVs together with the supporting documents, and Reports of Collections and Deposits with Official Receipts and validated deposit slips to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006, Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA and Section 34 of COA Circular 92-382.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate and Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs together with the supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation reports for her cash advances.

We also recommended that the LCE coordinate with the NGAs concerned regarding the required terms and provisions to be incorporated in the MOA, specifically the timelines and/or schedules of implementation of the projects for proper monitoring of the progress of PPAs and evaluating the implementation of the projects and the utilization of funds for the purpose intended.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs together with the supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the LCE direct the designated Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the DVs and supporting documents and to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.
We also recommended that the chairman and members of the BAC and BAC Secretariat refund the excess amount of honoraria received by them.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.    95-006, by submitting to the Auditor the required DVs with complete documentation.
We also recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.

We recommended that the LCE direct the employees concerned to submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Reports of Official Travels in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control of gasoline, oil and lubricant expenditures.

We recommended that reconciliation of balances between the Cashbooks and the General Ledger be made on a regular basis so that corrections/ adjustments that may surface can be readily made to ensure the fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements. 


We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare suggested journal entries to adjust/correct the under/ overstatements of the Cash-Disbursing Officer account.

We recommended that the              OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer undertake the reconciliation of the General   Ledger and the   Cashbook, on a regular basis, to ensure prompt, accurate, and complete recording     of entries/data for the fair presentation of financial statements. 



We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exercise due diligence in recording collections and deposits in the Cashbook to ensure the accuracy and completeness of data capture therein.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
Reconcile the accounts Due to NGAs and Due to other LGUs and identify the details of the unreconciled balances and the negative balances of the accounts; and
Make necessary adjustments to correct the erroneous entries affecting the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts to ensure reliable presentation of the year-end balances in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate to reconcile their records relative to the LDRRMF-Special Trust Fund balance and identify the amount included therein that were allotted for Capital Outlay expenditures.
We also reiterated our recommendations and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
Transfer the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE from CYs 2019 to 2023 to the Special Trust Fund and record under the Trust Liability-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books;
Revert the unutilized LDRRMF from CYs 2017 and 2018 totaling ₱3.181 million to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and make it available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the Sangguniang Bayan; and
Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the transfer and reversion of the unexpended balances of LDRRMF.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to submit on time all the records of transactions with supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant-Designate to facilitate the timely preparation of the financial statements and other required reports.
Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare and submit to the Auditor the complete set of Financial Statements, its supporting schedules, and other financial reports within the prescribed period as required by P.D. No. 1445 to timely verify and examine the financial condition and operating performance of the Municipality as basis for rendering the appropriate audit opinion.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer, agreed to ensure that the 20% DF be optimally utilized by fully implementing the various programs, projects and activities included in the AIP to help achieve the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the Municipality for the benefit of its constituents.
We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Submit prior approval or authorization from the Sanggunian for the LCE to enter into a contract for the project, proposed 50 units, 5m in height Solar Streetlights as required under Section 22(c) of RA No. 7160, considering that the details of the project in Annual Investment Plan, Improvement of Streetlights and Installation of Municipal Directional Signages were not specified;
Direct the concerned personnel to submit a justification on the variance noted between the Report on the Status of Utilization of the 20% DF and the 4th Quarter Utilization Report submitted by the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to the DOF-BLGF and submit quarterly reports on the utilization of 20% DF to the Audit Team; and
In coordination with the Municipal Development Council:
Review the unimplemented PPAs charged against the current and continuing appropriations and determine whether such PPAs are still feasible; otherwise, reprogram it to implementable PPAs that would timely serve the purpose of the 20% DF; and
Ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and implementation ready.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the MDRRMO, agreed to properly plan, give emphasis and prioritize the timely implementation of LDRRM PPAs identified in the LDRRMFIP in order to address and strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Issue the Contract Lease, providing the specific conditions of the contract, with the market stall occupants;
Direct the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to determine the correct amount of unpaid and delinquent market stall rentals and strictly imposed its collections; and
Direct the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to find ways to increase the percentage of occupancy of the vacant market stalls.
We also reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, agreed to fast track the update and revision of the Municipal Revenue Code in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and DILG-DOF JMC No. 2019-01.
We further reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all imposed taxes, charges and fees are supported with duly enacted revenue ordinances.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the concerned official to submit the justification on the noted deficiencies and the lacking supporting documents as enumerated in Annex M, and henceforth, ensure that the disbursements of government funds be supported with adequate documentation.
We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure the proper awarding of contracts, adhering strictly to the pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to:
Release the shares of the Barangays from the Basic Real Property Tax within five days within the end of each quarter in compliance with Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160; and
Reconcile their records concerning the barangay RPT shares to ensure that correct amounts are remitted to the respective barangays.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Direct the personnel concerned to refund the amount paid as membership fee in a private organization; and
Refrain from allowing the said membership fee of the employees of the Municipality to a private organization to be charged against the government funds and adhere to the provision of DBM NBC No. 563 dated April 22, 2016.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant - Designate to submit the following:
Lacking paid DVs for CY 2023 and CY 2022 transactions with supporting documents totaling ₱2.894 million, as shown in Annex N;
Proof of cancellation of 90 checks, as shown in Annex O; and
Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms.
Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure that the submission of disbursement vouchers and supporting documents thereto is made promptly and observe strictly the provisions of Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and the pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
Submit the lacking supporting documents enumerated in the attached Annexes P and Q to establish the propriety and validity of the transactions;
Ensure that the details of the post-qualification process undertaken by the Municipality be provided in the Post Qualification Evaluation Summary Report to determine whether the bidder complied with the requirements specified in the bidding documents; and
Ensure that all disbursements must be supported with complete documentary requirements before processing the payment thereof to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Direct the concerned personnel to submit the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB), duly reviewed and endorsed by the DILG to the Audit Team and to follow strictly the provision of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
Direct the GAD Focal Point person to attend seminars/trainings necessary in the fulfillment of her duties; and
Together with the GAD FPS, ensure the full implementation of GAD PPAs identified, utilize the allocated budget for GAD to maximize the funds and ensure that the relevant GAD PPAs will address/respond to the gender-related issues identified in the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
Determine the breakdown of the prior years’ balance of the account Due to BIR;
Remit the tax withheld in CY 2023 and in the prior years’ tax; and
Henceforth, properly monitor the taxes withheld and remit intact the full amount to the BIR within the prescribed period pursuant to the provisions of Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 and Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations and the Municipal mayor agreed to:
a) 	Direct all the concerned Department Heads to formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations embodied in the previous years’ audit report; and
Designate specific personnel to consolidate the formulated action plan using the AAPSI form and submit it to the Audit Team.
Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to, that within sixty (60) days from its receipt of the Annual Audit Report, submit to the Audit Team the duly accomplished AAPSI with supporting documents, and likewise furnish the DBM, the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a copy of the said report and ensure that said status report is posted in the Municipality’s website.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the concerned personnel to submit the justification on the use of the BGCM despite the lapse of the validity period of the fund as specified under DBM Circular Letter No. 2021-13 dated December 31, 2021.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct: 

The concerned RHU personnel to prepare the complete Inventory Report on the remaining undistributed vaccines and other donated supplies as at year-end with information as to valuation, and furnish a copy thereof to the Office of the Municipal Accountant-Designate; and

The Municipal Accountant-Designate to record the transaction on the agency books based on the complete Inventory Report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-009.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor submit justification on the:

Hiring of the Technical Consultant for the period October 16, 2021 to                  April 15, 2022, April 16, 2022 to June 30, 2022, and September 1, 2022 to                  February 28, 2023; and

Award of the contract which extended beyond the fiscal period CY 2021 and             CY 2022, contrary to Section 7.6 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

Henceforth, we maintain our recommendation that the Municipality discontinue the hiring of private lawyers as Technical Consultant through a Contract of Service pursuant to COA Circular No. 98-002 and RA No. 7160, otherwise payments therefor will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to renew her fidelity bond pursuant to the provisions of P.D. No. 1445, R.A. No. 7160 and Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to safeguard government funds and properties from to risk of non-recovery due to loss, improper or unauthorized use and misappropriation thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Consolidated Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and submit it to the Auditor together with the individual RAAFs of all accountable officers in compliance with the provisions of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

The Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Reports of Checks Issued are prepared in accordance with the prescribed format provided in the Local Treasury Operations Manual and are duly accomplished.

The Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure that the Reports of Checks Issued are duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer, check the reports against the submitted disbursement vouchers and supporting documents, and acknowledge receipt thereof.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant-Designate and Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid disbursement vouchers with complete supporting documents and the cancelled checks to the Auditor, and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended and the management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Accountant-Designate record the receipt and issuance of inventories under the appropriate inventory and expense accounts;
b. Supply Officer ensure that issuances of inventory are supported with Requisition Issue Slips, and to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued to the Municipal Accountant-Designate; and
c. Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories at year-end to determine the undistributed/unconsumed items and prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories that must be submitted to the COA Office not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended and the management agreed to submit the liquidation reports and necessary supporting documents to the IPG for the fund transfers received that are already due for liquidation and refund the unused or excess amount in compliance with the MOA.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to analyze, reconcile and identify/trace the details of transactions debited or credited to Government Equity and prepare the adjusting entries to close its balance and recognize the appropriate liability account/s.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
a. Review/analyze prior years’ transactions pertaining to unidentified discrepancies, prepare a reconciliation report and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts; and
b. Conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments of reconciling items periodically, as identified and substantiated, to prevent existence of long outstanding unadjusted reconciling items.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate and Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs together with the supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid DVs and their corresponding supporting documents to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the LCE direct the designated Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the DVs and supporting documents and to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.
We also recommended that the chairman and members of the BAC and BAC Secretariat refund the excess amount of honoraria received by them.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006, by submitting to the Auditor the required DVs with complete documentation.
We recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.

We recommended that reconciliation of balances between the Cashbooks and the General Ledger be made on a regular basis so that corrections/adjustments that may surface can be readily made to ensure the fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exercise due diligence in recording collections and deposits in the Cashbook to ensure the accuracy and completeness of data capture therein.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer undertake the reconciliation of the General Ledger and the Cashbook, on a regular basis, to ensure prompt, accurate, and complete recording of entries/data for the fair presentation of financial statements. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exercise due diligence in recording collections and deposits in the Cashbook to ensure the accuracy and completeness of data capture therein.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Create an Inventory Committee to fully conduct the physical count of all PPE and
prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of the following
year;
b. Require the Property/Supply Officer-Designate and Municipal Accountant-
Designate to prepare, maintain and update the Property Cards (PC) and PPE
Ledger Cards for all PPE accounts; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to determine the specific details of the
undepreciated PPEs and recognize depreciation expense for all depreciable PPE
items.
We further recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipality will avail
of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-
006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and
accountability.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to account for all her undeposited collections and
deposit the same with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB); and
b. Henceforth, the concerned accountable officers to deposit all collections intact
and in full amount with the authorized depository bank in accordance with the
policies and procedures under PD No. 1445 and NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume
I.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. The concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund
immediately their unliquidated cash advances;
b. The Municipal Accountant to ensure that no cash advances shall be allowed to
any official or employee unless the previous cash advance granted is liquidated;
and
c. The Municipal Accountant to look into the details of the negative balances
included and effect the necessary correction to accurately record the balance of
the unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-
Designate and the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the paid disbursement vouchers
with complete supporting documents and the cancelled checks to the Auditor, and to
adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006 and Section
7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the Management agreed that
the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Planning and Development Office
and Municipal Engineering Office, ensure that the 20% Development Fund be
optimally utilized by implementing the various programs, projects and activities
included in the AIP to help achieve the desirable socio-economic targets and
outcomes of the Municipality for the benefit of its constituents.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a. In coordination with the Municipal Development Council review the
unimplemented PPAs charged against the continuing appropriations and
determine whether such PPAs are still feasible, otherwise, reprogram it to
implementable PPAs that would timely serve the purpose of the 20% DF; and
b. Ensure that quarterly reports on the utilization of the 20% Development Fund are
prepared and submitted to the concerned government agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Designate a responsible person to facilitate the processing of the Certificate of
Title of the 14 parcels of land and those other lots donated to the Municipality that
were not yet transferred in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the
interest of the Municipality over those properties; and
b. Create an Inventory and Appraisal Committee to accurately identify all the lands
owned by the Municipality and provide proper valuation thereof to correctly
record the Land account in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the members of the
MDRRM Council, set up an emergency responder team and ensure that the planned
PPAs for disaster risk reduction and management are fully implemented to achieve
the maximum benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
process the return of the unutilized balance of BGCM fund amounting to ₱6.658
million to the Bureau of Treasury.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned officials to
comply with the posting and reporting requirements on the utilization of BGCM fund
as mentioned in the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the
municipal officials and employees concerned to observe strictly the pertinent
provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001, by submitting the scanned copies of
contracts, purchase orders, with complete details and supporting documents via
e-mail to the Auditor within five working days from the execution thereof and submit
the hard copy afterwards.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned payees to refund the
excess amount of the COVID-19 Active Hazard Duty Pay and Special Risk Allowance
totaling ₱12,181.13 they respectively received and henceforth ensure that payments
are made within the prescribed limits to avoid incurrence of irregular expenditures.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor propose to
the Sanggunian the creation of the mandatory positions in the MDRRMO and to
provide funds thereof in accordance with the guidelines and provisions set by
NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4,
2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer properly accomplish, update daily and
certify all her cashbooks pursuant to Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the New Government
Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units, Volume II to
ascertain the correctness and completeness of the transactions recorded thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to renew
her fidelity bond pursuant to the provisions of P.D. No. 1445, R.A. No. 7160 and
Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to safeguard government funds and properties from to
risk of non-recovery due to loss, improper or unauthorized use and misappropriation
thereof.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor provide a
separate room or safe cubicle for the Liquidating Officer and Disbursing Officer,
respectively. The partition of which must be appropriately enclosed and given
corresponding lock and must ensure that only authorized personnel are allowed to
have access to the room in order to protect government funds and property against
risk of loss through theft.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the
Consolidated Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and submit
it to the Auditor together with the individual RAAFs of all accountable officers in
compliance with the provisions of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
1. The Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Reports of Checks Issued are
prepared in accordance with the prescribed format provided in the Local Treasury
Operations Manual and are duly accomplished.
2. The Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure that the Reports of Checks Issued
are duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer, check the reports against the
submitted disbursement vouchers and supporting documents, and acknowledge
receipt thereof.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to submit on-time all the
records of transactions with supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant-
Designate so as to facilitate the timely preparation of the financial statements and
other required reports.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit to the Auditor the Trial Balances and
complete set of Financial Statements of the Municipality within the prescribed period
as required by PD No. 1445 to aptly verify and examine the financial condition and
performance of the Municipality as basis for rendering the appropriate audit opinion.

1.15   We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the (a) BAC to conduct strict eligibility screening of the suppliers before awarding the contract;  and  (b) Municipal Accountant (MA) to (i) require strict compliance  with the documentary requirements on  procurements undertaken through Negotiated Procurement – Shopping by ensuring that the necessary supporting documents attached to DVs are complete and in order prior to payment; and (ii)  BAC and MA to comply with the posting and  reporting requirements on the utilization of the BGCM  pursuant to the requirements prescribed under RA No. 9184 and its 2016 Revised IRR, and all relevant policies issued by the GPPB.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Health Officer to (a) submit the summary of vaccinated list of sex-disaggregated vaccine-recipients as to the classification of COVID-19 vaccination priority population groups for audit; and (b) designate a focal person for the COVID-19 Vaccination Program and deploy encoders to focus on the preparation and updating of reports.

We recommended that the LCE require the MT to continuously comply with the policy on the remittance of collections daily and intact to avoid the accumulation of cash in her custody as embodied in Section 32 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
We recommended that the LCE require the MA to consistently prepare accurate BRS in a timely manner for fair presentation of the Cash in Bank accounts of the Municipality in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE (a) create an inventory committee to conduct the physical count of PPE to avail of the “One-Time” Cleansing of PPE accounts following the prescribed regulations under  COA Circular No. 2020-006; (b) require the MA to (i) derecognize the small value items in the PPE accounts in the books; (ii) recognize subsequent purchases of similar nature as inventories upon purchase and treat them as an expense upon issuance to the end-user covered with the necessary ICS; (iii) properly establish, adjust and reflect the accurate balance of the Buildings account; and (iv) execute a Memorandum of Agreement and Deed of Donation to document the transfer of ownership of barangay structures from the books of Municipality to the barangay books of accounts; and (c) instruct the Municipal Administrator and Property  Officer  to facilitate the immediate transfer of the land titles under the name of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MA to (i) properly record the procurement of supplies and materials as inventory account pursuant to the  Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; (ii) maintain supplies ledger cards by type/kind of supplies/materials and reconcile the ledgers with the records of the Supply Officer and the report on the actual physical count; (b) require the Supply Officer to perform his mandated  duties and responsibilities on  the receipt, issuances, monitoring of inventory items, and the preparation and submission of the required reports pursuant to the existing regulations; and (c) require the concerned officials/employees to reconcile the balances of the inventory accounts per books and to submit the necessary RIS and SSMI to reflect the accurate balance of the Inventory accounts.

We recommended that  the LCE require the LDC through the Municipal Engineer (ME) and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to (a) properly plan, monitor, and evaluate all developmental projects to pave the way for the timely implementation and completion of  planned PPAs and effective utilization of funds; and (b) ensure the regular monitoring of  the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions such as initiating  replacements or alternatives  to the infeasible projects may be introduced at the earliest time.

We recommended that the LCE (a) require the MSWDO to prepare the necessary reports  such as the Brief summary of the reported program or project; copies of reported policy issuances; and results of HGDG tests to support the GPB and AR submitted to the DILG; (b) require the concerned offices to develop and update the  GAD database as basis for the planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation, and implementation of the GAD PPAs; (c) require the GFPS to update the GAD Code in accordance with pertinent regulations; and (d) establish a GAD M&E by issuing an Executive Order or Administrative Order creating the M&E Team pursuant to the JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all its PPE and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of the following year;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate and Supply Officer to update and maintain the specific Subsidiary Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for all PPE of the Municipality; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate and Supply Officer to reconcile the amounts recorded in the books with the RPCPPE to establish the correctness and reliability of the balances of PPE accounts reported in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipality adhere to the specific guidelines mentioned in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 in the conduct of physical count of PPE.
d. Direct each office/department of the Municipality to conduct inventory-taking and tagging using stickers with control numbers and record all PPE existing in their respective offices and same shall be validated by the Inventory Committee to facilitate the preparation of the property cards, PPE ledger cards and Inventory Report by the GSO, Municipal Accountant and Inventory Committee, respectively.
(AAR 2015-2019)

We recommended the following:
a. The Municipal Treasurer and other concerned municipal officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their unused cash advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 339 of RA No. 7160 in relation with Section 89 of PD No. 1445, and COA Circulars Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances; and
b. The Municipal Accountant-Designate to adhere to pertinent provisions and ensure that no cash advances shall be allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance granted is settled or a proper accounting thereof is made, which she agreed to comply with.
c. The LCE to direct the imposition of appropriate sanctions for the unjustified failure of the concerned Municipal officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances; (AAR 2015-2019)
d. The LCE to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to acknowledge and record liquidations of cash advances upon submission of complete supporting documents equivalent to the amount granted. If the money advanced is not fully utilized, require refund of the unspent amount evidenced by an Official Receipt before recording the settlement; (AAR 2015-2019)
e. The LCE to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records relative to the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of unliquidated cash advances; (AAR 2015-2019)
f. The LCE to direct the Accountable Officers to use the cash advance solely for the specific legal purpose for which it was granted and not incur expenses in excess of the authorized amount granted; (AAR 2015-2019)
g. The LCE to direct the concerned Municipal officials to follow the guidelines and procedures set in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the write-off, if warranted, of the unliquidated cash advances which have been long outstanding/remained dormant in the books of accounts; and
h. The LCE to direct the conduct of an investigation and take appropriate action on the ORs issued by the Municipal Treasurer, representing refunds to settle her outstanding cash advances, which were dated December 17, 21 and 29, 2015, but appeared accomplished only after several ORs with various dates for the month of February 2016 were issued. (AAR 2015-2019)

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineering Office ensure that the proposed program under the BGCM be implemented as soon as possible to timely deliver the vital services needed in addressing the crisis caused by the COVID-19 pandemic in the Municipality.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineering Office ensure that the 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized by implementing the various programs, projects and activities included in the AIP to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality for the benefit of its constituents.

We recommended and the management agreed that the concerned Department Heads will formulate an action plan to implement the audit recommendations and will submit immediately the AAPSI form to the audit team, and likewise will furnish the DBM, the Speaker of the House of Representatives, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a copy of the said report and will ensure that said status report is posted in the Municipality’s website as mandated by Section 99 of R.A. No 11260. We also recommended that a properly accomplished AAPSI be submitted to the COA Auditor within 60 days from receipt of the AAR, either in printed form or by way of electronic document.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned municipal officials to:

b. Transfer the Municipality’s unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books, and discuss the details of such in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned municipal officials and employees to ensure the full utilization of the allocated budget and propose plans/programs for GAD to maximize the funds and benefits thereof and ensure that the relevant PPAs and GAD activities will address/respond to the gender issues identified.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned personnel to properly mark all Municipality-owned vehicles with “For Official Use Only,” under which shall be written the name of the Municipal Government and the office operating or using the same.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to verify the validity and existence of the Inter-agency Receivables, and determine its composition, so that necessary adjusting entries can be taken up in the Municipality’s books of accounts and appropriate actions on the collection of these receivables can be made, if identified. We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit all the paid DVs together with the supporting documents, and Reports of Collections and Deposits with Official Receipts and validated deposit slips to the COA Auditor and to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006, Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA and Section 34 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate and Municipal Budget Officer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and PPSAS Nos. 1 and 24, by submitting the complete set of financial statements, to include the SCBAA and supporting disclosure of an explanation of the reasons for material differences, if any, to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: For all of the above deficiencies, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE): (a) instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Finance Committee to assess the fair value of the two lots acquired thru donation for eventual recognition in the books in accordance with IPSAS No. 17; (b)  direct the Municipal Accountant to properly establish, adjust and reflect the accurate balance of the Buildings account, and facilitate the transfer of barangay structures from the books of Municipality to the barangay’s books of accounts; (c) create an Appraisal Committee to assess the value of all unserviceable property of the Municipality for their proper disposal and derecognition from the books; (d) establish an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual physical inventory to determine the actual existence and condition of the PPE; and (e) require the Property Officer to prepare/maintain Property Cards, and reconcile the same with the records in the Accounting Office in order to establish the accuracy of the account balances as of year-end.

We recommended that the LCE: (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert vigorous efforts to work back on previous years’ records to facilitate proper resolution of the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts without details amounting to ₱14,321,027.97 and ₱3,457,801.72, respectively; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to request the abstract of RPT collections made by the PGB in previous years for eventual remittance to concerned barangays; (c) require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe regulation on the remittance of the barangay shares on a quarterly basis within five (5) days after the end of each quarter; and (d) compel the Heads of the RHUs to provide documents or make an agreement on the disposition and allocation of the PhilHealth Capitation and to utilize the fund pursuant to the regulations.

We recommended that the LCE: (a) require the full compliance of the contractors with the Scope of Work/Terms of Reference; and (b) to strictly review and thoroughly inspect the work undertaken by the contractors before the issuance of the Certificate of Completion/Acceptance; and (c) ensure that the future programs and projects implemented by the Municipality be efficiently and effectively used to maximize the use of government funds pursuant to laws and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require: (a) the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the contract duration and to properly review the request for extensions in accordance with the regulations; (b)  the concerned officials to properly notify and furnish copies of the suspension and/or resumption order and the grant of work extension to the Audit Team for its timely review; (c) the Municipal Engineer to periodically monitor completed projects within the defects liability period to allow the contractor to rectify any deficiencies; and (d) impose liquidated damages to the Contractor, if warranted, pursuant to Section 8.1 of Annex “E” of R.A. No. 9184.
We likewise recommended that the LCE require the continuous implementation of all mandated targets embodied its Annual Investment Plan to effectively provide the public the intended benefits from the developmental projects.

We recommended that the LCE require the MDRRM Officer to: (a) cause the planning and preparation of the LDRRMFIP with the objective of preparing for, and mitigating the risks in the event of disasters and calamities; (b) include in its LDRRMFIP the programming of PPAs for the unexpended prior years LDRRMF; and (c) identify in detail the PPAs to be charged for the current and previous year’s LDRRMF.

We recommended that the LCE: (a) provide attention to the 24 remaining barangays on their compliance relative to the establishment of their own MRF; (b) require the barangays with MRF structures to actively participate in the solid waste management activities; (c) embark on effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program through re-use, recycling, and composting activities for an effective solid waste diversion as embodied in its SWM Plan; and (d) facilitate the immediate purchase of generator to make use of all idle and unutilized equipment at the Central MRF, or allow other barangays with stable electrical supply to make use of the equipment.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the BIR regulations on the withholding of the correct amount of taxes due, and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to be compliant with the remittance of mandatory contributions due to GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG for the employees to immediately enjoy their benefits.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to (a) require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory; and (b) ensure that succeeding transactions comply with the existing regulations to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) shall:


    a. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical inventory
       of the Municipality’s properties, and  to  prepare  and  submit  the
       RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant-Designate and  Auditor,  pursuant
       to the pertinent provisions of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs;


    b. Designate a GSO and direct him/her to prepare the required  property
       records, such as Property Cards  and  Acknowledgement  Receipts  for
       Equipment issued to concerned accountable officers; and


    c. Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to maintain and update the
       PPELCs  for  proper  identification  of  all  PPEs   and   reconcile
       accounting records with the submitted RPCPPE  in  order  to  present
       accurate and reliable PPE balances in the financial statements.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:


    a. The Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare the BRS within 10 days
       after receipt of bank statements and submit the same monthly to  the
       Auditor  pursuant to Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular  No.
       96-011;


    b. The Municipal Accountant-Designate and Municipal Treasurer to ensure
       coordination between their offices for timely submission of all  the
       necessary documents to facilitate the proper and regular preparation
       of the BRS that will support the Cash in Bank balances; and


    c. The Municipal Accountant-Designate  and/or  Municipal  Treasurer  to
       secure/ request print-out or snap-shot copy of the  bank  statements
       from the authorized government depository bank after the end of each
       month for the timely preparation of the BRS.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE direct:


    a.  The  Municipal  Treasurer  to  account  for  all  her   undeposited
       collections and reconcile her records with that  of  the  Accounting
       Office to properly reflect the amount of Cash Local Treasury in  the
       Financial Statements;


    b. The Accountable Officer to deposit all her collections  intact  with
       the authorized depository bank daily or  not  later  than  the  next
       banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections
       8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382; and


    c. The Municipal Accountant-Designate to  maintain  subsidiary  ledgers
       for  the  accounts  of  all  collecting/accountable   officers   and
       reconcile these with their records regularly.

Recommendations:


    We recommended the following:


    a. The Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct  the  concerned  Municipal
       officials and employees to liquidate and/or refund immediately their
       unused cash advances pursuant to the provisions of Section 339 of RA
       7160 in relation with Section 89 of PD No. 1445, and  COA  Circulars
       Nos. 97-002 and 96-004, to avoid accumulation of  unliquidated  cash
       advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant  accounts
       in the financial statements;


    b. The LCE to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate  to  adhere  to
       pertinent provisions and ensure  that  no  cash  advances  shall  be
       allowed to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance
       granted is settled or a proper accounting thereof is made;

    c. The LCE to direct the imposition of appropriate  sanctions  for  the
       unjustified  failure  of  the  concerned  Municipal  officials   and
       employees to liquidate their cash advances;


    d.  The  Municipal  Accountant-Designate  to  acknowledge  and   record
       liquidations of cash advances upon submission of complete supporting
       documents equivalent to the amount granted. If the money advanced is
       not fully utilized, require refund of the unspent  amount  evidenced
       by an Official Receipt before recording the settlement; and


    e.  The  Municipal  Accountant-Designate  to  reconcile  their  records
       relative to the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash  advances
       and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the  balances  of
       unliquidated cash advances.

       No. 1445,  and  COA  Circular  Nos.  97-002  and  96-004,  to  avoid
       accumulation of unliquidated cash advances  and  to  attain  a  fair
       presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements;


    b. The LCE to direct the imposition of appropriate  sanctions  for  the
       unjustified  failure  of  the  concerned  Municipal  officials   and

       reconcile their records  relative  to  the  grant,  utilization  and
       liquidation of cash advances;

       liquidation of cash advances upon  presentation  of  sufficient  and
       appropriate documents evidencing payment of expenses  equivalent  to
       the amount granted. If the money advanced  is  not  fully  utilized,
       require refund of  the  unspent  amount  evidenced  by  an  Official
       Receipt before recording the settlement.


    e. The Accountable Officers to use the  cash  advance  solely  for  the
       specific legal purpose for  which  it  was  granted  and  not  incur
       expenses in excess of the authorized amount granted;

       guidelines and procedures set in COA  Circular  No.  2016-005  dated
       December  19,  2016  on  the  write-off,  if   warranted,   of   the
       unliquidated cash advances which have been long outstanding/remained
       dormant in the books of accounts.

    a. The LCE, as Chairman, and all the other  members  of  the  Municipal
       Development  Council  (MDC)  prepare  the  AIP  containing  priority
       development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF, to include only
       specific and identifiable programs/projects/activities  (PPAs)  with
       corresponding quantifiable targets, attainable  within  the  desired
       time frame, consistent with the guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-
       1;  Moreover, the MDC and other heads  of  offices  shall  regularly
       monitor the progress of the projects, their  timely,  effective  and
       efficient implementation, and  the  commitment  from  the  concerned
       offices to implement the PPAs as planned; and
    b. Ensure that the  20%  DF  is  optimally  and/or  fully  utilized  in
       accordance with the  guidelines  to  achieve  the  desirable  socio-
       economic development and  environmental  outcomes  to  maximize  the
       benefits for the constituents.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE, as Chairperson, together  with  the  other
    members of the LDRRM Council, comply  with  the  disaster  preparedness
    minimum standards on equipage to adequately  prepare  the  Municipality
    when hazards/disasters strike.

    We  also  recommended  that  the  LDRRMF  be  fully  utilized  for  the
    implementation of projects  and  activities  contained  in  the  LDRRMF
    Investment Plan for the year to achieve the maximum  benefits  for  the
    Municipality’s constituents.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE direct the  Municipal  Accountant-Designate
    to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance  of  the  LDRRMF,  which
    includes the balances of the QRF and the DRRMF – MOOE, to the STF under
    the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books,  and  discuss
    the details of such in the Notes to the FS pursuant to COA Circular No.
    2012-002.


    We also recommended the maintenance of a separate bank account for  the
    LDRRMF where all the accumulated unutilized funds will be deposited  so
    that in case adversities may befall, the Municipality’s needs shall  be
    fully funded by the available cash under the LDRRMF in the Trust Fund.

Recommendations:

    We recommended that the LSB prepare the  Annual  School  Board  Budget,
    supported by PPAs, and the Municipality disburse the SEF in  accordance
    to the provisions of Sections 99 and 100 of RA No. 7160 and  DepEd-DBM-
    DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.


    We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate prepare the
    quarterly  and  annual  reports  on  SEF  utilization  for   promoting,
    enhancing  and  monitoring  transparency  and  accountability  in   the
    allocation and utilization of the SEF.

Recommendation:

    We  recommended  that  the  LCE  direct  the  concerned  officials  and
    employees to strictly comply with the requirements prescribed  in PCW,
    DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and to  maximize  implementation  of
    GAD related programs and activities that will address gender issues and
    concerns in the Municipality.

Recommendations:

    We recommended that the LCE  establish  the  Municipality’s  LCPC  that
    advocates child rights, plans  and  initiates/recommends  interventions
    and monitors PPAs for children in the locality.


    We  also  recommended  that  the  LCE  and  other  officials  concerned
    appropriate 1% of the  IRA  in  the  Municipality’s  Annual  Budget  as
    required by RA No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No.  2012-120  for
    the strengthening and implementation of the PPAs of the LCPC.

Recommendation:

    We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned personnel  to  observe
    strictly the pertinent provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and COA
    Circular No. 95-006 on the timely, complete and  proper  submission  of
    required reports.

Recommendations:

    We recommended  that  the  LCE  direct  all  the  concerned  Department
    Heads/Division Chiefs to  submit  their  action  plans  and  status  of
    implementation on the CY 2016 AAR  recommendations  including  that  of
    prior years, related/connected to their departments/divisions  and  the
    information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the  AAPSI,
    and submit the same to the COA Auditor.


    We also recommended that a properly accomplished AAPSI be submitted  to
    the COA Auditor within sixty (60) days from the  receipt  of  the  AAR,
    either in printed form or by way of electronic document.
