observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all her collections intact and within the prescribed period with the AGDB pursuant to Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
We also recommended that the LCE may designate a liquidating officer, whenever necessary, in order to lighten the workload of the Municipal Treasurer and give her more time to update and reconcile her cashbooks and to prepare the monthly Reports of Collections and Deposits, the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms and other required reports.
Further, we recommended that the LCE direct the conduct of an investigation and take appropriate action on the issuance of ORs with various dates in December 2015, but appeared accomplished only after several ORs were issued in February 2016.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials and employees, especially the Municipal Treasurer, to liquidate and/or refund the unused cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular Nos. 96-004, 97-002 and 2012-004, to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts in the financial statements. Likewise, no additional cash advance shall be granted to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated pursuant to the aforecited provisions of law and regulations of the COA.
We also recommended that the LCE approve only the grant of cash advance for a legally authorized specific purpose.
Further, we recommended that the LCE may designate a Cash Disbursing Officer, whenever necessary, in order to give more time to the Municipal Treasurer to update and reconcile her cashbooks and to prepare and submit the liquidation reports to settle her unliquidated cash advances.
Further, we recommended that the LCE direct the conduct of an investigation and take appropriate action on the ORs issued by the Municipal Treasurer, representing refunds to settle her outstanding cash advances, which were dated December 17, 21 and 29, 2015, but appeared accomplished only after several ORs with various dates for the month of February 2016 were issued.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials and employees in-charge/assigned in the implementation of the SWM Program in the Municipality to adhere strictly to the provisions of RA No. 9003.
We also recommended the following: Require the strict implementation of waste segregation at source; Implement separate collection schedules for specific types of wastes, and ensure that such shall cover all the barangays in the Municipality; Observe the use of separate compartment/container with cover for specific types of wastes to ensure the containment of solid wastes while in transit; Coordinate with Barangay Officials regarding information dissemination/ campaign on proper segregation and disposal of wastes to be implemented in the barangays; Make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to revise the Municipal Ordinance to include the imposition of garbage fees as required under RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials and employees to take immediate actions, as follows: Prepare the GPB in accordance with the prescribed procedures to ensure that gender issues identified are the existing concerns/problems in the Municipality, and that planned PPAs address/ respond to such gender issues. Likewise, prioritize the PPAs that address the more urgent gender issues; Prepare and submit the GAD AR in accordance with the procedures and format prescribed under JMC No. 2013-01 and support it with the necessary documents; Strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

We recommended that the LCE, as Chairman, and all the other members of the Municipal Development Council (MDC), observe the guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 in formulating the PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF, and to include only specific and identifiable PPAs with corresponding quantifiable targets attainable within the desired time frame. We also recommended that the 20% DF be strictly utilized according to the aforecited guidelines in order to maximize the benefits for the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended/ unobligated balance of the LDRRMF, which includes the balance of the QRF and the DRRMF – MOOE, to the STF under the account Trust Liability – DRRM in the Trust Fund books, and discuss the details of such in the Notes to the FS pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002. We also recommended that the LCE, as the Chairman of the MDRRMC, together with the other members of the Council, adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, especially in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP and submission of the required DRRM reports.

We recommended that the LCE direct the employees concerned to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001, by submitting copy of the MOA and other required documents to the Auditor for transactions involving fund transfers to properly evaluate the projects. We also recommended that the LCE coordinate with the NGAs concerned regarding the required terms and provisions to be incorporated in the MOA, specifically the timelines and/or schedules of implementation of the projects for proper monitoring of the progress of PPAs and evaluating the implementation of the projects and the utilization of funds for the purpose intended. Further, we recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to request from the bank, copy of the credit advice and other documents pertaining to the amount credited to the Municipality’s account, as basis to identify and confirm with the source agencies the purpose of the fund transfer and the provisions for its proper utilization and implementation of the projects. Likewise, it will be the basis for the proper accounting and reporting in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned department heads to be particular in adhering to the conditions provided in the MOA, contracts or other agreements with regard to the transfer of funds, for prompt, efficient and effective implementation of projects and programs to avoid loss of opportunities on the part of the beneficiaries. We also recommended that the concerned department heads coordinate with the NGAs, taking full responsibility of the transactions and making sure that the projects are implemented as planned/programmed.

We recommended that the LSB prepare the Annual School Board Budget, supported by PPAs, and the Municipality should disburse the SEF pursuant to the provisions of Sections 99 and 100 of RA No. 7160 and Section 1 of RA No. 5447. The required reports for the budget preparation be in the prescribed format under the Budget Operations Manual for LGUs. Further, we recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation reports for her cash advances.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and PPSAS Nos. 1, 2 and 24, by submitting the financial statements and the accompanying notes and other supporting disclosure requirements, and other related documents to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and Designated Municipal Accountant to release the shares of the barangays from the proceeds of the basic RPT, including the interest thereon, within the prescribed period which is five (5) days after the end of each quarter, pursuant to Section 271 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the Bank Statements. We also recommended that the Designated Municipal Accountant secure print-out or snap-shot copy of the bank statements as an alternative procedure in preparing the BRS on time. Further, we recommended that the LCE provide additional staff to the Designated Municipal Accountant to assist her in the preparation of the financial reports and supporting schedules/documents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to correct the proper classification of accounts based on the account descriptions provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009. Likewise, the subsidiary ledgers or detailed schedules of working papers shall be submitted to support the conversion and/or reclassification of accounts in order to ascertain the correctness of its presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE, as chairperson of the LDRRM Council, with all its members observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF. We also recommended that the fund must be strictly utilized in accordance with the approved AIP of the Municipality for a given year.

We recommended that the LCE create its Municipal GFPS that shall take the lead in mainstreaming GAD in LGU PPAs. The GFPS shall adhere strictly to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the preparation of the annual GPB and GAD AR.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006, by submitting to the Auditor the required DVs with complete documentation.

We recommended that the concerned LGU officials strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA No. 7160 and DBM BC No. 2014-03. They should see to it that payments of incentives to government personnel be based on laws, rules, and regulations authorizing payments of such to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances. We also recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.

Likewise, we recommended that the inventory reports be reconciled with the PPE account balances in the financial statements and that the appropriate PPE accounts are used thereon.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government to purchase and install a safety vault or steel cabinet with vault where collections can be kept before they are remitted at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and to provide additional security features at the Office of the Market Master to safeguard their collections.

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to (a) submit the deficient documents; (b) strictly follow the Joint Circular on the release, use, and reporting of the Confidential Fund; (c) ensure the submission to the Commission on Audit the cash advance voucher together with the transmittal and its supporting documents within seven (7) days the after release of the check; and (d) maintain the Cash Disbursement Record.

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to (a) speed up the preparation of documents necessary for the registration of the lots in the Municipality’s name with the Register of Deeds; (b) exert best efforts to make the necessary research for the retrieval of documents supporting the untitled parcels of land including the basis for the valuation of the lots; and (c) refrain from constructing/improving structures in lots that are not owned by the Municipal Government of San Rafael.

We recommended that Management take immediate steps to dispose of all unserviceable properties to prevent possible loss and avoid further deterioration in accordance with the guidelines provided by the COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.

We recommended that Management (a) require the Municipal Finance Committee to include in its budget the insurance expenses of all insurable properties of the Municipality; and (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the submission of the updated list of inventory of insurable properties with the GSIS so that the necessary insurance coverage can be applied with the GSIS insurance fund.

We recommended that Management (a) strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 on the invitation of observers for all stages of the procurement process and on the use of Competitive Bidding to ensure transparency in the procurement process; and (b) submit the relevant documents to establish the validity of the claims for payment.

We recommended that Management comply strictly with the aforementioned regulation and require the Municipal Engineering Office and other officials concerned to disclose all transactions involving PPAs for transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the (a) Members of the SB consider enacting appropriate ordinance on the regulation of the operations of three-wheeled public conveyance to prevent undesirable outcomes as a result of unregulated proliferation of tricycles along the Municipality’s highways, which may pose hazards and imminent danger to pedestrians and motorists’ lives and properties; and (b) strictly implement the various ordinances on traffic guidelines.

We recommended that Management (a) direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineer to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that timely necessary interventions may be made for the attainment of the objectives of the programs; (b) direct the other responsible officials concerned to address the issues that impede the full implementation of priority projects; (c) continue to invest in high–impact or developmental projects and include in the Annual Investment Plan; and (d) cause the re-appropriation of the balance of the fund in the ensuing year to finance development projects for the benefit of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that Management (a) require the LDRRMO, through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to be certified correct by the Local Accountant and submitted to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and (b) continue to allocate 5% of the budget for the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and utilize it in accordance with the guidelines on the matter.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste management and activate the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continual implementation of the existing environmental laws; (b) continuously intensify information campaign and educate the constituents on the necessity of  establishment of MRF in every barangay; (c) consider extending financial assistance, resources, and technology to the 33 barangays which have remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. 9003; and (d) take appropriate action to provide adequate supply of electricity at the Material Recovery Facility (MRF) of the Municipality so it could operate efficiently and effectively.
We recommended that Management continue to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 on the remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management direct the (a) Accounting Office and the Human Resource Office to tally their records of premiums withheld and remitted; and (b) Accounting Office and Treasury Office to invariably comply with the full remittance of withheld premiums and government share on or before the 10th of the following month in compliance with Section 6 of R.A. No. 8291.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a
    complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s  properties,  prepare
    the inventory reports of  all  PPE  and  submit  said  reports  to  the
    Auditor, Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer.


    We also recommended that the inventory reports be reconciled  with  the
    accounting records in  order  to  present  accurate  and  reliable  PPE
    balances in the financial statement. Further, we recommended  that  the
    Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer prepare and update the
    PPE  ledger  cards  and  property  cards,  respectively,   for   proper
    identification of all PPE.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer  to  deposit
    all her collections intact and within the prescribed  period  with  the
    AGDB pursuant to Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA
    Circular No. 92-382.


    We also recommended that the LCE may designate a  liquidating  officer,
    whenever necessary, in order to lighten the workload of  the  Municipal
    Treasurer and give her more time to update and reconcile her  cashbooks
    and to prepare the monthly Reports of  Collections  and  Deposits,  the
    Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms  and  other
    required reports.

Recommendations:


    We  recommended  that  the  LCE  direct  the  concerned  officials  and
    employees, especially the  Municipal  Treasurer,  to  liquidate  and/or
    refund the unused cash advances within the prescribed  period  pursuant
    to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular  Nos.  96-
    004, 97-002 and 2012-004, to avoid accumulation  of  unliquidated  cash
    advances and to attain a fair presentation of the relevant accounts  in
    the financial statements. Likewise, no additional cash advance shall be
    granted to any official or employee unless the  previous  cash  advance
    has been liquidated pursuant to the aforecited provisions  of  law  and
    regulations of the COA.


    We also recommended that the LCE approve only the grant of cash advance
    for a legally authorized specific purpose.


    Further, we recommended that the LCE may designate  a  Cash  Disbursing
    Officer, whenever  necessary,  in  order  to  give  more  time  to  the
    Municipal Treasurer to  update  and  reconcile  her  cashbooks  and  to
    prepare and submit the liquidation reports to settle  her  unliquidated
    cash advances.

Recommendations:

    We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated-Municipal  Accountant
    and  Municipal  Treasurer  to  submit  all  the  paid  DVs  and   their
    corresponding supporting documents to the COA  Auditor  and  to  adhere
    strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA  Circular  No.  95-006  and
    Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.

Recommendations:


    We  recommended  that  the  LCE  direct  the  concerned  officials  and
    employees     in-charge/assigned  in  the  implementation  of  the  SWM
    Program in the Municipality to adhere strictly to the provisions of  RA
    No. 9003.

    We also recommended the following:

     a. Require the strict implementation of  waste segregation at source;
     b. Implement separate  collection  schedules  for  specific  types  of
        wastes, and ensure that such shall cover all the barangays  in  the
        Municipality;
     c. Observe the use of separate compartment/container  with  cover  for
        specific types of wastes to ensure the containment of solid  wastes
        while  in  transit;
     d.  Coordinate   with   Barangay   Officials   regarding   information
        dissemination/ campaign  on  proper  segregation  and  disposal  of
        wastes to be implemented in the barangays;
     e. Make representation  with  the  Sangguniang  Bayan  to  revise  the
        Municipal Ordinance to include the imposition of  garbage  fees  as
        required under RA No. 9003.

Recommendations:


    We  recommended  that  the  LCE  direct  the  concerned  officials  and
    employees to take immediate actions, as follows:


     a. Prepare the GPB in accordance  with  the  prescribed  procedures  to
        ensure   that   gender   issues   identified   are   the    existing
        concerns/problems  in  the  Municipality,  and  that  planned   PPAs
        address/respond to such gender  issues.   Likewise,  prioritize  the
        PPAs that address the more urgent gender issues;
     b. Prepare and submit the GAD AR in accordance with the procedures  and
        format prescribed under JMC No. 2013-01  and  support  it  with  the
        necessary documents;
     c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of JMC No.  2013-01  of  the  PCW,
        DILG, DBM and NEDA.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE, as Chairman, and all the other members  of
    the  Municipal  Planning  Development  Council  (MPDC),   observe   the
    guidelines of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 in formulating the PPAs to be
    funded out of the 20% DF, and to include only specific and identifiable
    PPAs with corresponding  quantifiable  targets  attainable  within  the
    desired time frame. By this, the Municipality may be able  to  maximize
    the purpose of the 20% DF which will have an immediate  impact  on  its
    socio-economic development.


    We also recommended that the 20% DF be strictly utilized  according  to
    the aforecited guidelines in order to maximize  the  benefits  for  the
    Municipality’s constituents.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal  Accountant
    to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance  of  the  LDRRMF,  which
    includes the balance of the QRF and the DRRMF – MOOE, to the STF  under
    the account Trust Liability – DRRM in the Trust Fund books, and discuss
    the details of such in the Notes to the FS pursuant to COA Circular No.
    2012-002.

    We also recommended that the  LCE,  as  the  Chairman  of  the  MDRRMC,
    together with the other members of the Council, adhere strictly to  the
    provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002, especially in the  preparation
    of the LDRRMFIP and submission of the required DRRM reports.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE direct the employees concerned  to  observe
    strictly the pertinent provisions of  COA  Circular  No.  2012-001,  by
    submitting copy of the MOA and other required documents to the  Auditor
    for transactions involving fund  transfers  to  properly  evaluate  the
    projects implementation.


    We also recommended that the LCE coordinate  with  the  NGAs  concerned
    regarding the required terms and provisions to be incorporated  in  the
    MOA, specifically the timelines and/or schedules of  implementation  of
    the projects  for  proper  monitoring  of  the  progress  of  PPAs  and
    evaluating the implementation of the projects and  the  utilization  of
    funds for the purpose intended.


    Further, we recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer  to
    request from the bank, copy of the credit advice  and  other  documents
    pertaining to the amount credited to  the  Municipality’s  account,  as
    basis to identify and confirm with the source agencies the  purpose  of
    the fund transfer and the provisions for  its  proper  utilization  and
    implementation of the projects. Likewise, it will be the basis for  the
    proper accounting and reporting in the books of the Municipality.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned department heads to be
    particular in adhering to the conditions provided in the MOA, contracts
    or other agreements with regard to the transfer of funds,  for prompt,
    efficient and effective implementation  of  projects  and  programs  to
    avoid loss of opportunities on the part of the beneficiaries.


    We also recommended that the concerned department heads coordinate with
    the NGAs, taking full responsibility of  the  transactions  and  making
    sure that the projects are implemented as planned/programmed.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LSB prepare the  Annual  School  Board  Budget,
    supported by  PPAs,  and  the  Municipality  should  disburse  the  SEF
    pursuant to the provisions of Sections 99 and 100 of RA  No.  7160  and
    Section 1  of  RA  No.  5447.  The  required  reports  for  the  budget
    preparation be in the prescribed format  under  the  Budget  Operations
    Manual for LGUs.


    We also recommended that the LSB focus the utilization of  the  SEF  in
    providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of  quality
    education  by  public  elementary  and   secondary   schools   in   the
    Municipality.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant,  Municipal
    Budget  Officer  and  Municipal  Treasurer  to  observe  strictly   the
    provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445 and PPSAS Nos. 1, 2 and 24, by
    submitting the financial statements  and  the  accompanying  notes  and
    other supporting disclosure requirements, and other  related  documents
    to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

Recommendations:


    We  recommended  that  the  LCE  direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and
    Designated Municipal Accountant to release the shares of the  barangays
    from the proceeds of the basic RPT,  including  the  interest  thereon,
    within the prescribed period which is five (5) days after  the  end  of
    each quarter, pursuant to Section 271 of RA No. 7160.

Recommendations:


    We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned  Department  Heads
    to submit their action plans and status of  implementation  on  the  CY
    2015 AAR findings and recommendations including that  of  prior  years,
    related/connected to their departments  and  the  information  gathered
    therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit  the  same
    to the COA Auditor.


    We also recommended  that  properly  accomplished  AAPSI  be  submitted
    within sixty (60) days from their receipt of the AAR to the COA, either
    in printed form or by way of electronic document.

We recommended that management (a) direct the Municipal Treasurer to abide strictly with the NGAS regulations governing check disbursements so that only those processed checks actually released to payees are reported in the RCI/Check Register as basis for recording disbursements during a particular accounting period; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV for the adjustment of the unreleased checks amounting to P546,132.22 tocorrectly state the balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts in the Agency’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
(a) All checks actually released to payees were recorded in the Report of Checks Issued (RCI). The Municipal Treasurer agreed to submit to Accounting Department the daily report of checks issued.

(b) Since the checks were already released to respective payees as early as Jan. 2016, the Municipal Accountant deemed that it may not
be practical anymore to prepare the adjustment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) cause strict compliance by the Agency with the DOF Circular for purposes of promoting better fund management; and (b) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer the immediate transfer of cash balances from the existing private bank depository accounts to an authorized government depository bank within the prescribed deadline to avoid the imposition of sanctions by the DOF/BangkoSentralngPilipinas (BSP) as mentioned in the aforementioned Circular.
observations.managementAction: The accounts maintained at Postal Bank and Philippine Veterans Bank were already closed by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly supervise the preparation and submission of certified list of taxpayers and list of tax delinquencies as basis for the recognition of RPT/SET Receivables in the books of the Municipality; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Treasurer every start of the year to ensure accuracy and promptness in recording the Receivables.
observations.managementAction:
(a) The Municipal Treasurer strictly supervised the preparation and submission of certified list of taxpayers to the Accounting Office.

(b) The Municipal Accountant already requested for the list of receivables from the Treasurer’s Office and also prepared the necessary entry.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management (a) advise the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method without exception; (b) immediately provide safe central storage for purchases of inventories in CY 2016 and onwards; and (c) properly orient the newly-designated staff on the prescribed reports and records relative to supply management. In addition, we recommended the recognition in the books of accounts of some unused supplies and materials worthP114,956.88 by debiting Inventories account and crediting the appropriate Expense accounts.
observations.managementAction:
(a) For CY 2016, the Municipal Accountant started to adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method for all the supplies and other inventory items purchased.

(b) Management provided storage for inventories at the back of the Municipal Gymnasium.

(c) Per verification, stock cards and Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (MRSMI) were not maintained by the designated personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make representations with the Register of Deeds (R.D.) for the immediate titling of the land to protect the interest of the government and, at the same time, ensure that the Land account presented in the financial statements constitutes duly registered real properties.
observations.managementAction: The 2,439 sq.m lot was transferred in the name of the Municipality of San Rafael as evidenced by Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) No. 039-2016003809.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) advise the  Municipal Budget Officer and the Local School Board to efficiently plan programs/projects/activities to ensure that all related expenses are appropriately classified and recorded in accordance with the Chart of Accounts;  (b) undertake careful deliberation with project implementing offices/departments during the budget process to obtain details of anticipated expenses for the budget year, thereby avoiding the practice of lumping the expenses to Other Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses;and (c) refrain from allowing the re-alignment from OMOOE to other expenses without SB or LSB resolution as required under R.A. 7160 or the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction:
(a) The Municipal Budget Officer coordinated with the different department heads to efficiently plan their program/projects and activities for the proper classification of accounts during budget deliberation.

(b) The Municipal Budget Officer conducted budget deliberation of the PPAs of the different officer/department.

(c) All funds re-alignment was submitted by the Municipal Budget Officer to the SB/LSB for approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) limit the payment of representation expenses within reasonable amount; (b) for economic reasons, stop the practice of spending for meals, except during official entertainment of guests/visitors; and (c) refrain from funding meetings or gatherings conducted by partner organizations especially so when these are considered as inherent in their regular functions.
observations.managementAction:
(a and b) The management assured us that they will see to it that payment of representation expenses is within the reasonable amount.

c) The Municipal Budget Officer coordinated with various department heads regarding the hosting and meetings/gatherings and will no longer provide funding for the meetings/ gatherings of partner organizations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the procedures prescribed by R.A. No. 9184 before entering into lease contract with private entities/individuals to ensure that the terms and conditions agreed upon are generally advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: The management has stopped the practice of entering into lease contract with private entities without first revisiting the procedure prescribed in RA. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) direct the Municipal Treasurer to require the Accountable Officer to abide strictly with the preparation of the Report of Disbursements (RD) for each nature of cash advance granted to her; and(b) advise the Municipal Accountant to ensure that liquidations are covered by a complete report based on the format prescribed by the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction:
(a) For the year 2016, the Cash Disbursing Officer prepared the Report of Disbursements.

(b) The Report of Disbursements in the prescribed format were submitted to the Accounting Department together with the paid payrolls and cash disbursement registers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive a) continue to invest in high-impact undertakings to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development fund and refrain from venturing into non-capital investment projects under the Fund: and b) ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within the targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervisionby the municipal officials in-charge; thereby taking full advantage of the developmental benefits.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Management continued to invest in high-impact projects out of the 20% Development Fund.

(b) Management closely monitored the implementation of the projects. However, several planned projects were unimplemented as of year-end.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 9.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Members of the Municipal Local School Board (a) prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization; (b) refrain from accommodating expenditures on activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; (c) request for inputs or feedbacks from the DepEd officials/School District Supervisors/ Schools Division Superintendent in theprioritization of SEF projects and expenditures and ensure that PPAs are in accordance with the DepEd criteria and the provisions of laws, rules, and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; (d) maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its primary goals and objectives; and (e) monitor closely the implementation of the PPAs and take appropriate remedies, suchas realignment or re-programming of funds, if necessary, thereby retaining in the Annual Budget only those relevant and priority activities in consultation with the school heads/project proponents.
observations.managementAction:
(a) The LSB prepared a budget. However, some of the items were not in compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on the utilization of the SEF.

(b) The Municipal Budget Officer discussed, with the members of the LSB, the expenses that can be allowed in SEF budget and will no longer accommodate expenditures in activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute.



(d) Management maximized the utilization of the SEF.

(e) Management closely monitored the implementation of the PPAs out of the SEF.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 11.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) continually adopt sound measures to prevent and mitigate all kinds of risks in the eventuality of a disaster or calamity; (b) advise the LDRRMO to strictly follow the LDRRMFIP based on the four thematic areas to ensure that the entire Municipality is well-prepared for acts of God and man-made disasters; (c) properly account for donations received from all sources, whether in cash or in kind, for purposes of promoting transparency and accountability; and (d) instruct the Municipal Accountant/ Municipal Budget Officer/ Municipal Treasurer to promptly prepare/submit the year-end status report on the utilization of LDRRMF and require them to transfer any fund balance not later than December 31 of each year.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Management continued to adopt sound measures to prevent and mitigate all kinds of risks in the eventuality of a disaster or calamity.

(b) Management instructed the LDRRMO to strictly follow the LDRRMFIP.

(c) Donations, if any, were properly accounted.

(d) The unutilized balance was transferred in the following year.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 10.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) cause the stoppage of payment of membership dues and contributions to all organizations/associations which are not recognized as local leagues under the Local Government Code; (b) hold members personally liable for the unpaid dues effective 2016; (c) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to obligate funds strictly for the league of municipal mayors,vice mayors, and other local elective officials as explicitly provided for in Section 258 of R.A. No. 7160; and (d) as an alternative, develop an in-house human resource development program catering to the training needs of all department heads/key personnel.  In such manner, their professional growth could be assessed fairly in terms of the goals andobjectives of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the payment of membership dues and contributions to all organizations not recognized as local leagues.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive and the MENRO (a) involve closely the barangay officials in solid waste managementand activate the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees in every barangay for the strict enforcement and continual implementation of the existing environmental laws; (b) intensify information campaign on the mandatory segregation of wastes at source and establishment of MRF in every Barangay; and (c) extend financialassistance, resources and technology to the six barangays which have remained non-compliant to the basic requirements of R.A. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
(a) The Municipality continued to conduct monthly cleanup of high ways and de-clogging and irrigation canals. The members of the Sangguniang Barangay, Municipal Employees, NGOs, and selected students from different schools participated in this activity.

(b) The MENRO coordinated with the Barangay Officials the conduct of information campaign and the necessity of the establishment of MRF. However, the reason for the non-compliance of the barangay in the establishment of an MRF was due to lack of funds andland.

(c) Some financial assistance were provided to the barangay.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 11.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) promptly prepare the remittance vouchers based exactly on the amounts withheld to avoid retention of substantial balances under the Due to BIR account and prevent possible imposition of penalty; and (b) undertake online remittances to the BIR on the basis of duly approved DVs/supporting documents.
observations.managementAction:
(a) The Municipal Accountant promptly prepared the remittance vouchers. However, the beginning balance of the Due to BIR account was not yet remitted.

(b) Remittance of taxes withheld was supported with DVs and remitted through the e-payment system of the BIR.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Finding No. 13.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the reviewing/certifying officials concerned, such as the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Budget Officer to avoid incurrence of audit suspensions and disallowances by complying strictly the existing laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The certifying officials concerned assured the Audit Team that they will comply with the existing laws, rules and regulations to avoid incurrence of NS/ND in the future.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS monthly to the Auditor, within ten (10) days after receipt of the BS.
We also recommended that the Designated Municipal Accountant secure print-out or snap-shot copy of the BS as an alternative procedure in preparing the BRS on time.
Further, we recommended that the LCE provide additional staff to the Designated Municipal Accountant to assist her in the preparation of the financial reports and supporting schedules/ documents.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Only some BRS for the Trust Fund were submitted to the Auditor.
Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to correct the proper classification of accounts based on the account descriptions provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009. Likewise, the subsidiary ledgers or detailed schedules of working papers shall be submitted to support the conversion and/or reclassification of accounts in order to ascertain the correctness of its presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE, as Chairman of the Municipal Planning and Development Council, with all its members observe the guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF. We also recommended that the fund must be strictly utilized according to the guidelines in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE, as chairperson of the LDRRM Council, with all its members observe the guidelines of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in formulating PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF. We also recommended that the fund must be strictly utilized in accordance with the approved AIP of the Municipality for a given year.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE create its Municipal GFPS that shall take the lead in mainstreaming GAD in LGU PPAs. The GFPS shall adhere strictly to the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the preparation of the annual GPB and GAD AR.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
The Municipality has created its GFPS only in February 2017, and the Office Order is still for signature of the LCE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the provisions of RA No. 9003, especially the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Management explained that the establishment of a sanitary landfill is very costly, but they have adopted an alternative procedure by using a temporary landfill to cover the facility and to control the volume of wastes in the area.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the provisions of DBM BC No. 2004-5A in the granting of honoraria to government personnel involved in government procurement. Likewise, any excess in the amount collected from the sale of bid documents at the end of the year shall form part of the local government funds.
We also recommended that the chairman and members of the BAC and BAC Secretariat refund the excess amount of honoraria received by them.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented
Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445, and COA Circular No. 95-006, by submitting the financial reports/accounts together with all the supporting documents to the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
There are still financial reports and documents not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Designated Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to observe strictly the provisions of Section 122 of PD No. 1445, and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006, by submitting to the Auditor the required DVs with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
DVs and supporting documents relative to the audit observation are still not submitted to the COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU fully utilize the 20% DF by implementing all priority development programs, projects and activities as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan pursuant to Section 287 of RA No. 7160 and DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 to enhance the general welfare of its constituents.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
For CY 2016, majority of the 20% DF budget were counterpart funds required by various NGAs for the implementation of collaborated projects, but these were not utilized as of year-end because no funds were received from the concerned NGAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE designate a General Services Officer and direct the employees concerned to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Section A.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 by submitting a copy of all contracts and POs within five (5) working days from its execution, and notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from the acceptance of such deliveries.
observations.managementAction:
Partially Implemented
Only contracts for infrastructure projects were submitted to the COA, but not within the prescribed period.
